User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the UniEats application, including clients (students, faculty, administrative staff) and internal personnel (cashiers, kitchen staff, administrators).

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The institutional email address of the user, used for login.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., Client, Cashier, KitchenStaff, Admin).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Categorizes menu items for better organization and browsing (e.g., 'Main Courses', 'Beverages', 'Desserts').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the menu category.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single item available for order on the cafeteria's menu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item.

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item, including ingredients or preparation.

price.description: The current price of the menu item.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the menu item.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this menu item belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N MenuItem)

isAvailable.description: Indicates whether the menu item is currently available for order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's online order for food and beverages.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User (client) who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

pickupTime.description: The chosen time for the customer to pick up the order.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Preparing', 'Ready for Pickup', 'Picked Up', 'Cancelled').

specialInstructions.description: Any special instructions or notes provided by the customer for the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated (e.g., status change).

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Details for a specific menu item included within an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem that was ordered. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the menu item ordered.

unitPrice.description: The price of the menu item at the time the order was placed.

customizationNotes.description: Specific customization requests for this item (e.g., 'no onions', 'extra cheese').

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a customer's rating and comment for a specific menu item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User (client) who left the review. (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem that was reviewed. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N Review)

rating.description: The rating given by the user, typically on a scale (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: The textual comment or feedback provided by the user.

reviewDate.description: The date and time when the review was submitted.

Ingredient.title: Ingredient
Ingredient.description: Represents a raw material or component used in the preparation of menu items, for inventory control.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ingredient entity.

name.description: The name of the ingredient (e.g., 'Milk', 'Coffee Beans', 'Lettuce').

unitOfMeasure.description: The unit of measure for the ingredient (e.g., 'grams', 'ml', 'units', 'kg').

currentStock.description: The current quantity of the ingredient available in stock.

minStockLevel.description: The minimum stock level for this ingredient, triggering low stock alerts.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the ingredient record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the ingredient record was last updated.

RecipeItem.title: RecipeItem
RecipeItem.description: Links a MenuItem to its constituent Ingredients, specifying the quantity of each ingredient required. This forms the recipe for inventory deduction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RecipeItem entity.

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem for which this is an ingredient. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N RecipeItem)

ingredientId.description: Reference to the Ingredient required for the MenuItem. (Relationship: Ingredient 1:N RecipeItem)

quantityRequired.description: The quantity of the ingredient (in its unit of measure) needed to prepare one unit of the menu item.

definition.description: Stores user profile details. The document ID (`userId`) must match the `request.auth.uid` and the `id` field within the document for path-based authorization.
Identifies users with administrative privileges. Presence of a document here grants admin role. Used for DBAC (Database Access Control) checks via `exists()`.
Identifies users with cashier privileges. Presence of a document here grants cashier role. Used for DBAC checks via `exists()`.
Identifies users with kitchen staff privileges. Presence of a document here grants kitchen staff role. Used for DBAC checks via `exists()`.
Defines menu categories. Publicly readable by all clients, writeable by users with admin roles.
Represents individual menu items. Publicly readable by all clients, writeable by users with admin roles.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a menu item.

definition.description: Reviews associated with a specific menu item. Includes denormalized `userId` of the reviewer for authorization independence, allowing review owner to modify. Publicly readable by all.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent menu item.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the review.

definition.description: Details linking a menu item to its constituent ingredients. Used for inventory tracking and recipe management. Readable/writeable by kitchen staff and administrators.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent menu item.

params.name: recipeItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recipe item.

definition.description: Inventory of raw ingredients. Readable/writeable by kitchen staff and administrators.

params.name: ingredientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ingredient.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all customer orders. Includes denormalized `userId` (owner) for client-specific authorization and `status` for efficient staff queries. Supports QAPs for listing orders by `userId` (client) or by `status` (staff).

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

definition.description: Details for individual items within a specific order. Includes denormalized `userId` (from the parent order) for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule checks without fetching the parent order. This ensures client and relevant staff can access order item details.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to facilitate real-time updates and shared access for orders across different user roles (clients, cashiers, kitchen staff), while strictly adhering to core security principles. Central to this is prioritizing **Authorization Independence** through strategic denormalization and **QAPs (Query as a Proxy for Security)** via structural segregation.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Crucially, to enable atomic security rules and avoid inefficient `get()` calls, authorization-relevant fields are denormalized:
*   **Orders (`/orders/{orderId}`):** Each order document includes the `userId` of the client who placed it. This allows rules to check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` directly, ensuring a client can only access their own orders without needing to fetch data from a parent `User` document. This also enables staff to query and manage orders based on status or pickup time without complex cross-collection lookups.
*   **Order Items (`/orders/{orderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`):** To maintain Authorization Independence even within subcollections, each `OrderItem` document will also contain the `userId` (or `orderOwnerId`) of the parent order's owner. This allows rules for `orderItems` to be atomic, checking `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` or `exists(/roles_kitchenstaff/$(request.auth.uid))` without needing to `get()` the parent `Order` document.
*   **Reviews (`/menuItems/{menuItemId}/reviews/{reviewId}`):** Each review includes the `userId` of the author, allowing atomic checks for ownership when modifying or deleting a review.
*   **DBAC (Database Access Control):** User roles (Admin, Cashier, KitchenStaff) are managed in dedicated top-level collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{userId}`). Authorization for staff actions involves simple, efficient `exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` checks, completely decoupling role authorization from hierarchical data dependencies.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure ensures that common list queries can be secured efficiently:
*   **Client Order History:** Clients can fetch their orders using `db.collection('orders').where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The `userId` field in the `Order` document ensures that security rules can validate this query efficiently without acting as a filter on an unindexed or overly broad dataset.
*   **Staff Order Management:** Cashiers and Kitchen Staff can list orders based on `status` (e.g., `db.collection('orders').where('status', '==', 'Preparing')`) or `pickupTime`. This is secured by requiring the querying user to have an appropriate role (e.g., `exists(/roles_kitchenstaff/$(request.auth.uid))`) and allowing the specific query pattern.
*   **Public Menu & Reviews:** `/categories` and `/menuItems` are designed for public read access, allowing efficient listing for all users. Reviews within `/menuItems/{menuItemId}/reviews` are also publicly readable, satisfying QAPs for displaying all reviews for a menu item.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   Data with different security postures is segregated. For example, public menu items are in `/menuItems`, while staff-only inventory is in `/ingredients` and `/menuItems/{menuItemId}/recipeItems`. User-specific data (like profile or owned orders) contains the `userId` for direct access control.

This design ensures that the application can efficiently display and manage orders across multiple devices and user roles, with a clear, robust, and easily debuggable set of Firestore security rules.
